{{Quote|Don't they know that painting the ship red only makes it a better target?|Jull Dremon, on the ''Radiant VII''|<ref name="The New Essential Guide to Vehicles and Vessels">''[[The New Essential Guide to Vehicles and Vessels]]''</ref>}}
[[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] '''Jull Dremon''' was a [[Neimoidian/Legends|Neimoidian]] who served as [[Chief Gunner/Legends|Chief Gunner]] aboard the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]] [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]] [[Saak'ak/Legends|''Saak'ak'']] during the [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|Invasion of Naboo]]. He relayed [[Viceroy of the Trade Federation|Viceroy]] [[Nute Gunray/Legends|Nute Gunray's]] orders to destroy the [[Radiant VII/Legends|''Radiant VII'']] to [[OWO-3]] and was later transferred to the [[Lucrehulk-class battleship/Legends|Droid Control Ship]] [[Vuutun Palaa/Legends|''Vuutun Palaa'']], [[Death/Legends|dying]] when it was destroyed during the [[Battle of Naboo/Legends|Battle of Naboo]].

==Biography==
[[File:Saakak_bridge.png|thumb|left|250px|[[Sil Unch]], [[Tey How/Legends|Tey How]], and Jull Dremon seated on the ''Saak'ak''{{'s}} bridge]]
By the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]],<ref name="Essential Reader's Companion" /> [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] Jull Dremon served as [[Chief Gunner/Legends|Chief Gunner]]<ref name="Rogues Gallery" /> aboard the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]] [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]], the [[Lucrehulk-class battleship/Legends|modified]] [[Lucrehulk-class LH-3210 cargo freighter/Legends|''Lucrehulk''-class LH-3210 cargo freighter]] [[Saak'ak/Legends|''Saak'ak'']]. Shortly before the [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|Invasion of Naboo]],<ref name="The Phantom Menace" /> Dremon relayed [[Viceroy of the Trade Federation|Viceroy]] [[Nute Gunray/Legends|Nute Gunray's]] orders<ref name="Rogues Gallery" /> to destroy the [[Republic Diplomatic Corps/Legends|Republic Diplomatic Corps]] [[Consular-class light cruiser/Legends|''Consular''-class space cruiser]] [[Radiant VII/Legends|''Radiant VII'']]<ref name="The Phantom Menace" /> to a [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droid]] [[Gunner/Legends|gunner]]<ref name="Lt. Antidar Williams">{{DB|character|ltwilliams|Williams, Lt. Antidar}}</ref> designated [[OWO-3]].<ref name="Rogues Gallery" /> Dremon was later assigned by Gunray to the [[Lucrehulk-class battleship/Legends|Droid Control Ship]] [[Vuutun Palaa/Legends|''Vuutun Palaa'']]<ref name="The Phantom Menace" /> when the ''Saak'ak'' left the [[Naboo system/Legends|Naboo system]],<ref name="Rogues Gallery" /> where the Chief Gunner was [[Death/Legends|killed]] when it was destroyed by [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] during the [[Battle of Naboo/Legends|Battle of Naboo]].<ref name="The Phantom Menace" />

==Personality and traits==
Just before ordering its destruction, Jull Dremon commented that the ''Radiant VII''{{'s}} [[Color/Legends|red]] livery only made it a better target.<ref name="The New Essential Guide to Vehicles and Vessels" /> A [[Neimoidian/Legends|Neimoidian]],<ref name="Rogues Gallery" /> he had mottled gray skin and red [[Eye/Legends|eyes]].<ref name="The Phantom Menace" />

==Behind the scenes==
Jull Dremon was originally created for ''[[The New Essential Guide to Vehicles and Vessels]]'', a [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] reference book written by [[W. Haden Blackman]]<ref name="The New Essential Guide to Vehicles and Vessels" /> and released on [[September 30]], [[2003]].<ref name="The New Essential Guide to Vehicles and Vessels release date">{{RandomHouseOld|url=catalog/display.pperl?isbn=9780345449023|text=Star Wars: The New Essential Guide to Vehicles and Vessels}}</ref> Dremon was [[Retroactive continuity|retconned]] into the [[1999]] film [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'']] by the [[Rogues Gallery (department)|Rogues Gallery]] recurring feature published in [[Star Wars Insider 147|''Star Wars Insider'' 147]],<ref name="Rogues Gallery" /> and his place on the right side of the ''Saak'ak'' and ''Vuunta Palaa''{{'s}} bridges was set in stone by [[Leland Chee]].<ref name="Creating & Naming">{{Facebook|username=star-wars-sourcebooks|url=notes/star-wars-sourcebooks/creating-naming-the-devious-neimoidians/676129629103814|name=Star Wars Sourcebooks|date=Jan 19, 2014|archivefile=File:NeimoidiansFacebook.pdf}}</ref>
